content:
- Final validation is complete: make check, make lint, make test, and live browser verification via Playwright Chromium against the Docker Compose API all passed.
- Key implementation result: examples/standalone-control-page.html now works from file:// because internal/httpserver adds centralized CORS/preflight headers without adding any template/static-serving path.
- Next after this context would be nothing for story-09; I am staging all files, committing with evidence in the message, pushing, and then stopping immediately.

- After the push, Ralph summary files changed again, likely due generated current task bookkeeping around the task switch.
- I am inspecting that diff now and will commit/push it too if needed so the remote head matches the actual workspace state.
- No product code work remains; this is just keeping Ralph metadata consistent.

- Read the previous progress log and loaded the active-task selection skill.
- Current task summary shows one active failing task: story-99 manual end-to-end verification, blocked by story-09 UI, and the blocker already appears done in current_tasks_done.
- Next I am refreshing Ralph task summaries, checking choose-task rules, and confirming the actual repo stack from the codebase before selecting the task.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the choose-task rules plus active/done summaries.
- Confirmed the actual codebase is a Go benchmark/control-plane service (`cmd/pg_gobench`, `internal/*`, `go test`, `go vet`, `gofmt`), not a copied template identity.
- There are zero active bug tasks and exactly one active failing non-bug task; its only blocker `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-01-standalone-html-control-page.md` already passes true, so story-99 manual verification is the mandatory next pick.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-99-manual-verify-everything/task-01-manual-verify-everything.md`.
- Reason: it is the only active unblocked failing task after blocker resolution, so it outranks meta-tasks and already-passing tasks under Ralph's strict precedence rules.
- Next after this context: execute the story-99 manual end-to-end verification task and then validate make check, make test, and make lint.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries + tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-99-manual-verify-everything/task-01-manual-verify-everything.md`.
- Next I am reading the task body to determine whether I must create/adjust a plan or execute an existing approved plan.

- Read the manual verification task body; it explicitly says this is a non-code manual verification task and to not use TDD or run make check/lint/test for this task.
- I am now compiling the complete feature inventory from all `.ralph/tasks/**/task-*.md` files so I can insert the checklist into the task file before any verification.
- After the checklist is in place, I will verify the product against real runtime surfaces: local binary/API, Docker Compose, scratch image, Kubernetes, metrics, docs, and standalone HTML.

- Inserted the generated manual feature checklist into the story-99 task file and marked the checklist-compilation acceptance item complete.
- Next I am reading the repo docs/examples/runtime entrypoints to design the lowest-friction manual verification sequence that still exercises every required surface.

- Marked the story-99 task in progress.
- Docker, kind, kubectl, actionlint, node, and cached Playwright Chromium are available in this environment.
- Next I am starting the documented Docker Compose stack because it covers the real scratch image, real PostgreSQL, mounted config/secrets, and the main HTTP API in one verification path.

- Started real manual verification against the Docker Compose stack and confirmed health/readiness/basic state/results/metrics plus JSON rejection paths.
- Found a real bug during the first stop-flow verification: after a user-triggered stop, `/benchmark/results` returns `status: "stopped"` but `stats.latest_error: "context canceled"`.
- Per the task rules I am stopping unrelated verification now, creating an add-bug task for this issue, and then switching Ralph to the bug task.

- Created `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled.md` with concrete Compose/curl reproduction and the expected stopped-run behavior.
- Running `.ralph/task_switch.sh` now so Ralph moves off the manual verification task and onto the newly created bug task.
- I also shut down the Compose stack after recording the reproduction so the workspace is left clean for the next context.
